evidence: risk_model.py contains four nearly identical train_*_risk_model functions (train_risk_model, train_tier1_risk_model, train_tier1_v1_risk_model, train_tier1_v1_design_model) that differ only in feature column lists and label extraction — ~60 lines of duplicated load-split-train-evaluate boilerplate per function

legacy/engine.py SimulationEngine has 5 methods (get_graph_at_step, iter_graphs, num_steps, network_graph, get_graph) that forward directly to self.adapter with zero policy or translation — pure delegation without value
Tier1RolloutSummary.to_dict() at tier1_rollout.py:230 is a one-line wrapper over dataclasses.asdict(self) with no transformation or filtering
metrics/__init__.py is a pure re-export facade (10 symbols, 0 defined) — this is an intentional package facade and appropriately skipped as a defect
HypatiaAdapter exposes domain-appropriate methods (generate_tles, calculate_isls, get_graph_at_step, iter_graphs) that compose real physics logic — abstractions in the core adapter are earned
tier1_rollout.py run_tier1_rollout is a well-bounded orchestration function that composes adapter, metrics, and failure sampling — no unnecessary indirection layers
gnn_model.py SatelliteGNN is a focused model class with no gratuitous base classes or protocols — direct implementation

issues.summary: Four near-identical train_*_risk_model functions duplicate ~60 lines of load-split-train-evaluate boilerplate each
evidence: train_risk_model (L235-283), train_tier1_risk_model (L164-225), train_tier1_v1_risk_model (L343-423), and train_tier1_v1_design_model (L426-507) all follow identical structure: load dataset, check class balance, train_test_split, fit RandomForestClassifier, compute metrics dict with accuracy/roc_auc/confusion_matrix/classification_report/feature_importances
The only varying axes are: (1) which feature columns to use, (2) how to extract the label series, and (3) which extra metadata to attach to the metrics dict
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared _train_rf_classifier(X, y, cfg, feature_names) -> Tuple[RandomForestClassifier, dict] that encapsulates split-train-evaluate-metrics. Each public function becomes a thin loader + call to the shared trainer. This reduces ~240 lines to ~60 and eliminates the risk of metric-reporting drift between variants.

issues.summary: SimulationEngine has 5 methods that forward to self.adapter with no added policy or translation
evidence: network_graph property returns self.graph_at_t0 which returns self.adapter.get_graph_at_step(0) — two-hop forwarding
get_graph() returns self.graph_at_t0 — another forwarding alias
issues.suggestion: Since legacy/engine.py is quarantined and deprecated, the cleanest fix is to remove the pass-through methods and have any remaining callers use HypatiaAdapter directly. If backward compatibility aliases are still needed, document which callers depend on them and set a removal date.

issues.summary: Tier1RolloutSummary.to_dict() wraps dataclasses.asdict() with no filtering or transformation
evidence: Line 230-232: def to_dict(self) -> dict: return asdict(self) — a one-line pass-through that adds no value over callers using asdict(summary) directly
issues.suggestion: Remove to_dict() and have callers use dataclasses.asdict(summary) directly. If the method exists to provide a stable API that might later add field filtering or renaming, add a comment documenting that intent.

root_causes: risk_model_boilerplate: The four training functions grew incrementally as new dataset versions (legacy, tier1, tier1_v1, tier1_v1_design) were added — each was likely copy-pasted from the prior variant without extracting the shared train-evaluate skeleton.

legacy_indirection: SimulationEngine was originally the primary entry point before HypatiaAdapter was promoted to a direct API. The delegation layer remains because it was quarantined rather than removed.
likely_symptoms: to_dict_trivial_wrapper is a minor symptom rather than a structural issue
possible_false_positives: metrics/__init__.py facade was flagged by the scanner (re_export_ratio=1.0) but is an intentional package facade stabilizing import paths — correctly skipped

gnn_model.py was flagged as a facade module (re_export_ratio=0.9) but the high ratio is due to standard library/framework imports (torch, GCLSTM, etc.), not re-exports — the module defines a real class
